prompt_text: Best way to run scenario analysis and board reporting without relying on spreadsheets for a CFOs and FP&A leads at growth-stage venture-backed companies team
response:
# Modern Scenario Analysis & Board Reporting Stack for Growth-Stage CFOs

## Core Problem with Spreadsheets
- Version control chaos
- Formula errors in critical models
- Manual consolidation of inputs
- Audit trail gaps
- Real-time collaboration friction

## Recommended Architecture

### **1. Planning & Modeling Layer**
**Primary options:**

- **Anaplan** (Workiva-owned) - Enterprise-grade, excellent consolidation, steep learning curve
- **Planful** (formerly Host Analytics) - Strong for mid-market, good UX
- **Mosaic** or **Pigment** - Built for modern finance, collaborative, good for scenario planning
- **Vimeo Create alternative**: **Causal** or **Tabla** - Lighter weight, formula-driven but cleaner than Excel

**Pick based on:**
- Team size (3-5 people → lighter tool; 10+ → enterprise)
- Complexity (single forecast vs. multi-entity consolidation)
- Integration needs (ERP connections, GL sync)

### **2. Data Integration Layer**
- **Integrate.io** or **Fivetran** - Automated ERP/GL pulls
- **Custom API** connections to your accounting system
- Avoid manual monthly data uploads

### **3. Presentation & Dashboarding**
- **Tableau** or **Looker** - Dynamic board dashboards
- **Sigma** - Lightweight, good for financial narratives
- **Metabase** - If you want open-source, embedded reporting
- **Mode Analytics** - SQL-friendly, collaborative

### **4. Scenario Management Workflow**

```
Input assumptions (Slack/tool forms)
    ↓
Automated model recalculation
    ↓
Real-time dashboard updates
    ↓
Narrative generation (AI-assisted)
    ↓
Board deck auto-population
```

## Practical Implementation Path (12-week)

### **Months 1-2: Foundation**
- [ ] Audit current model dependencies
- [ ] Implement Fivetran → data warehouse (Snowflake/BigQuery)
- [ ] Move actuals to central source of truth
- [ ] Choose planning tool (pilot with 2-3 scenarios)

### **Months 2-3: Model Migration**
- [ ] Rebuild key forecast models in chosen planning tool
- [ ] Link actuals → forecast waterfall
- [ ] Test 3-5 scenario branches
- [ ] Document assumptions in tool (not separate docs)

### **Months 3-4: Dashboarding**
- [ ] Build executive dashboard (Tableau/Looker)
- [ ] Scenario comparison views
- [ ] Variance analysis automation
- [ ] Board-ready visual library

## Specific Workflows to Automate

### **Monthly Board Prep**
```
1. Assumptions locked in planning tool (auto-notified stakeholders)
2. Model runs overnight
3. Dashboard refreshes + alerts on variances >5%
4. Narrative section auto-drafted (revenue drivers, risks, changes vs. prior month)
5. Export to deck template (Gamma or Beautiful.ai for polish)
```

### **Scenario Comparison**
- Version scenarios within tool (not separate files)
- Waterfall view: Base → Scenario A/B → Variance drivers
- Sensitivity tables auto-generated
- Risk scenarios tied to KPI thresholds

## Tool Combinations by Company Size/Complexity

### **$20-100M ARR (Lean FP&A)**
- Mosaic (planning) + Looker (dashboards) + Slack integration
- **Cost**: ~$20-30K/year
- **Lift**: Moderate (2-3 weeks to migrate models)

### **$100M+ ARR (Multi-entity)**
- Anaplan (planning) + Tableau (reporting) + Adaptive Insights
- **Cost**: $50-150K/year
- **Lift**: High (2-3 months, likely consulting needed)

### **Fast-Moving/Highly Experimental**
- Causal or Tabla (planning) + Sigma (reporting)
- **Cost**: $10-20K/year
- **Lift**: Low (collaborative, designed for scenario testing)

## Avoiding Common Pitfalls

❌ **Don't:** Buy enterprise tool for 3-person team  
✅ **Do:** Start
